reasoning: The question asks if Mahmoud Khalil will be deported before July 1, 2025. It is currently mid-May 2025, leaving only about 6 weeks until the deadline. While an immigration judge ruled on April 11, 2025 that he is eligible for deportation, Khalil is a lawful permanent resident with a high-profile legal team (ACLU, etc.) fighting the case. His lawyers immediately filed an appeal with the Board of Immigration Appeals (BIA), which grants an automatic stay of removal while pending. Additionally, he has an active federal habeas corpus challenge in a New Jersey federal court, where the judge ruled in late April to retain jurisdiction over his case. Legal experts predict the BIA and federal circuit court appeals will take anywhere from one to several years to resolve. Because of these ironclad procedural safeguards, ongoing litigation in multiple courts, the high-profile constitutional (First Amendment) nature of his defense, and the incredibly short 6-week timeframe, it is practically impossible for him to be legally deported before July 1, 2025.
